page: Amazon Web Services r6idn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs derived from 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8375C processor operating at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Storage is provided via 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity. The network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. While single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench falls into the average category, multi-core execution is highly competitive, with stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and PassMark CPU extended instructions scoring in the top tier (top 10%). Cryptographic operations also perform in the top tier, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AES and SHA_NI. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks consistently rank in the top 25%, though the overall PassMark memory score remains in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6idn.metal offers high resource density by combining bare-metal access, substantial memory capacity, and large local NVMe storage. However, workloads relying heavily on single-threaded execution may not fully leverage the hardware, as single-core benchmarks remain average. The absence of dedicated GPU hardware limits its utility for intensive deep learning training, though it achieves average to strong throughput in CPU-based LLM inference.

This instance is configured for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. It is suited for large-scale relational and non-relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, multi-threaded data compilation, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs from 64 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 200 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores in the top 10%, while single-core metrics remain in the average tier. Memory bandwidth is strong, placing in the top 25% for both read and write operations. This hardware profile delivers high resource density for memory-intensive applications, though the lack of a GPU limits accelerated computing tasks. The r6idn.metal is configured for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, multi-threaded data processing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 200 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized platform featuring high-capacity local NVMe storage and high-bandwidth networking for demanding database and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs via Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
1024 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses
Top-tier multi-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and multi-threaded data processing
